drill.title: U10 Possession to Attack in the Final Third

notes: Clarity: There is an inconsistency in the number of mini-goals. `organization.setupSteps` and the diagram specify one, while the `equipment` list and `goalMode` key reference two. Recommend using one central mini-goal to provide a clear, singular objective for the counter-attack.

Clarity: A minor conflict exists between the `organization.rotation` time ('Play for 4 minutes') and the `loadNotes` work interval ('90s work'). The 90-second work interval with a 1:1 rest ratio is superior from a sport science perspective for this age group and should be prioritized during execution.
Structure: The overall structure and detail, including specific role assignments and load management notes, are of exceptional quality and serve as a model for other drills.
qa.summary: An excellent, game-realistic practice for U10s focusing on final third possession and transition. The structure is clear, constraints are purposeful, and it aligns perfectly with its stated psychological and game model themes. A minor clarification on the number of counter-goals is needed.

area.notes: Use the width of the penalty area.

organization.scoring: Attackers score 1 point by scoring in the large goal. Defenders score 2 points by scoring in the counter mini-goal.
organization.restarts: After a goal or the ball goes out of play, the coach restarts with a pass to the attacking team.
organization.rotation: Play for 4 minutes, then rotate roles. Attackers become defenders, defenders rest, and resting players become the new attackers.
setupSteps: Mark out a 35x44 yard area, representing the attacking third of a half pitch.
Place one large goal with a goalkeeper at one end.
Place one mini-goal on the opposite end line for the defenders to counter towards.
Split players into a team of 4 attackers and 3 defenders, plus the GK (total 8 on field).
Position the attacking team with a striker, attacking-mid, and two wingers (from the 2-3-1).
Position the defending team with two center-backs and a defensive-mid.
Start the game with the coach passing the ball to an attacking midfielder.

json.setup: Set up 4v3+GK in Width of the 18-yard box to the halfway line (~44x35yd for U10). Coach at midfield with balls. Mark a wide channel (for winger isolation) and a cutback zone. Start from CAM on coach restart.
json.title: U10 Possession to Attack in the Final Third

coach.restart: Coach plays into CAM to start each 90s rep

loadNotes.rationale: Short work intervals preserve decision quality and avoid excessive fatigue for U10.
loadNotes.structure: 8 x 90s with 90s rest (1:1) — rotate groups briskly

roleUsage.notes: The Goalkeeper plays with the defending team. Roles are taken from the 2-3-1 formation.

teamShape.notes: The drill uses the attacking unit of a 2-3-1 formation against the central defensive block.

constraints: Controlled start: Defenders cannot press beyond the CAM’s starting line until after the CAM’s first touch.
Attacking team must complete at least 3 passes before shooting.
A goal scored with a one-touch finish is worth 2 points.
Defenders must try to score within 8 seconds of winning the ball.
json.description: A 4v3+GK game where attackers maintain possession in the attacking third to create and score goals. Defenders aim to win the ball and score in a counter-attacking mini-goal.

area.notes: Use the width of the penalty area.

organization.scoring: Attackers score 1 point by scoring in the large goal. Defenders score 2 points by scoring in the counter mini-goal.
organization.restarts: After a goal or the ball goes out of play, the coach restarts with a pass to the attacking team.
organization.rotation: Play for 4 minutes, then rotate roles. Attackers become defenders, defenders rest, and resting players become the new attackers.
setupSteps: Mark out a 35x44 yard area, representing the attacking third of a half pitch.
Place one large goal with a goalkeeper at one end.
Place one mini-goal on the opposite end line for the defenders to counter towards.
Split players into a team of 4 attackers and 3 defenders, plus the GK (total 8 on field).
Position the attacking team with a striker, attacking-mid, and two wingers (from the 2-3-1).
Position the defending team with two center-backs and a defensive-mid.
Start the game with the coach passing the ball to an attacking midfielder.

progressions: Add a recovering defender who starts on the end line and can join in once the attackers complete their first pass, making it a 4v4.
Remove the 3-pass minimum requirement to allow for faster, more direct attacks.
Add one recovering defender from halfway on coach signal → creates 4v4 momentary transition.
scoringHints: +1 bonus point for a goal scored by a third-man runner (e.g., CAM timing beyond the 9).
coachingPoints: Attacking Team: Create space by spreading out. Check your shoulders to see where pressure is and where teammates are.
Movement: Can you move into positions to receive a pass and see the goal?
Decision Making: Know when to pass to keep the ball and when to be brave and try to score.
Defensive Team: Work as a unit to pressure the ball. When you win it, can you look up and find a quick pass to the counter goal?
GK: starting position & communication on cutbacks; quick distribution on saves.

qa.summary: An excellent, game-realistic practice for U10s focusing on final third possession and transition. The structure is clear, constraints are purposeful, and it aligns perfectly with its stated psychological and game model themes. A minor clarification on the number of counter-goals is needed.
notes: Clarity: There is an inconsistency in the number of mini-goals. `organization.setupSteps` and the diagram specify one, while the `equipment` list and `goalMode` key reference two. Recommend using one central mini-goal to provide a clear, singular objective for the counter-attack.

Clarity: A minor conflict exists between the `organization.rotation` time ('Play for 4 minutes') and the `loadNotes` work interval ('90s work'). The 90-second work interval with a 1:1 rest ratio is superior from a sport science perspective for this age group and should be prioritized during execution.

fixDecision.reason: All QA dimensions are ≥4 → high quality, no fixer needed.
